title: Optical Diagnostics with Background Oriented Schlieren: A practical perspective on reactive and non-reactive flow scenarios

title: Review of background oriented schlieren and development for ballistic range applications

extract: plenoptic camera perspective views, circle of confusion / f-number sensitivity, cone-shock depth perception, limited viewing-angle range, relation to light-field BOS and single-camera tomography.

why: PI-DION directly targets inverse operators and studies physics-informed training without labeled target fields. It is the right advanced reference if real BOST has measurements and a trustworthy forward model but few paired 3D truths.
extract: Extract its inverse-problem formulation, unlabeled physics loss, stability assumptions, sensor/function representation and which assumptions fail for sparse-view BOST non-uniqueness.
access: Official ICLR 2025 OpenReview poster page and CC BY 4.0 PDF verified. Link-only entry; it is an advanced design reference, not the first T16 implementation.

title: Global field reconstruction from sparse sensors with Voronoi tessellation-assisted deep learning
authors: Kai Fukami, Romit Maulik, Nesar Ramachandra, Koji Fukagata, Kunihiko Taira
why: This is a foundational sparse-sensor-to-field baseline. Voronoi embedding preserves sensor location information and is a direct conceptual precursor to RecFNO's sparse-input design.
extract: Extract Voronoi tessellation input construction, sensor-layout experiments, super-resolution coupling, flow benchmarks and how camera-view measurements differ from point sensors.
access: Official Nature Machine Intelligence article verified. Link-only entry; use it for sparse-input representation rather than claiming it solves tomography.

title: Fourier Neural Operator with Learned Deformations for PDEs on General Geometries
authors: Zongyi Li, Daniel Zhengyu Huang, Burigede Liu, Anima Anandkumar
why: Geo-FNO shows how an FNO can work with non-rectangular domains by learning a deformation to a regular latent grid. It becomes relevant if the lab task cannot be represented by a trustworthy regular physics lift.
extract: Extract physical-to-latent deformation, inverse geometry handling, point-cloud/mesh inputs, interpolation baselines and the added failure modes compared with a fixed BOST lift.
access: Official JMLR landing page, open PDF and code link verified. Optional until real geometry makes a regular-grid lift inadequate.

title: Geometry-Informed Neural Operator for Large-Scale 3D PDEs
authors: Zongyi Li, Nikola Kovachki, Chris Choy, Boyi Li, Jean Kossaifi, Shourya Otta, Mohammad Amin Nabian, Maximilian Stadler, Christian Hundt, Kamyar Azizzadenesheli, Anima Anandkumar
venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
why: GINO combines graph and Fourier operators to move between irregular geometry and a compressed regular latent grid. It is a future route for ray/query geometry, not a reason to complicate the first undergraduate baseline.
extract: Extract SDF/geometry representation, graph-to-grid lifting, 3D memory strategy, discretization tests and the data/computing scale required before considering it for BOST rays.
access: Official NeurIPS 2023 abstract, paper and supplement links verified. Link-only advanced entry.

title: Multi-Grid Tensorized Fourier Neural Operator for High-Resolution PDEs
authors: Jean Kossaifi, Nikola Kovachki, Kamyar Azizzadenesheli, Anima Anandkumar
why: MG-TFNO addresses the memory and parameter cost that becomes dominant in high-resolution 3D operator learning. It is the scale-up reference after 24-cubed or 32-cubed FNO is proven useful.
extract: Extract domain decomposition, tensor factorization, compression ratio, local/global coupling, high-resolution protocol and whether accuracy is compared at equal compute.
access: Official NVIDIA publication page and the published TMLR PDF were verified. Link-only entry; standard FNO remains the first T16 baseline.

title: Real-time reconstruction of thermal fields from sparse sensors with a tensorized Fourier neural operator
authors: Wenyu Huang, Chunna Li, Jianjun Gou, Xiaobing Ma, Chunlin Gong
venue: International Journal of Heat and Fluid Flow
why: A recent hypersonic thermal-field example combining TFNO, random sensor masking, low-fidelity priors and short history. Its multi-fidelity design is relevant if OERF can provide CFD or classical reconstructions beside sparse measurements.
extract: Extract replication-masking, state/low-fidelity fusion, temporal-history input, sensor-layout generalization, latency measurement and the exact conditions behind reported errors.
access: Official Elsevier DOI and article metadata verified in July 2026. Publisher link only; this is a method neighbor, not an OERF paper.

title: U-NO: U-shaped Neural Operators
authors: Md Ashiqur Rahman, Zachary E. Ross, Kamyar Azizzadenesheli
why: U-NO adds a U-shaped multiscale and memory-efficient structure to neural operators. It is a credible second architecture if the small 3D FNO misses local thin-front structure or becomes memory-limited.
extract: Extract the resolution hierarchy, skip connections, 3D spatiotemporal experiment, memory accounting and which gains remain under matched parameter and training budgets.
access: Published TMLR OpenReview page and open PDF verified. Use after the standard FNO/CNN baseline, not as the first model.

title: Factorized Fourier Neural Operators
authors: Alasdair Tran, Alexander Mathews, Lexing Xie, Cheng Soon Ong
why: F-FNO uses separable spectral layers and improved residual connections to scale deeper. It is a useful efficiency and residual-design reference for a 3D BOST operator.
extract: Extract factorized spectral convolution, residual formulation, noise training, compute comparison and whether reported gains transfer from PDE rollout to inverse reconstruction.
access: Official ICLR 2023 OpenReview paper verified. Open PDF; no local cache added in this pass.

title: Convolutional Neural Operators for robust and accurate learning of PDEs
authors: Bogdan Raonic, Roberto Molinaro, Tim De Ryck, Tobias Rohner, Francesca Bartolucci, Rima Alaifari, Siddhartha Mishra, Emmanuel de Bezenac
venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
why: CNO is a function-space-consistent convolutional operator. It provides a more defensible local/multiscale comparison than calling an arbitrary voxel U-Net the only CNN baseline.
extract: Extract continuous-discrete consistency, resampling design, multiscale benchmarks, matched-budget protocol and high-frequency behavior.
access: Official NeurIPS 2023 proceedings page and open PDF verified.

title: Multiwavelet-based Operator Learning for Differential Equations
authors: Gaurav Gupta, Xiongye Xiao, Paul Bogdan
venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
why: The multiwavelet operator learns across repeated spatial scales and is a targeted candidate if Fourier truncation smooths thin flame fronts.
extract: Extract fixed wavelet filters, multiscale kernel representation, resolution tests, memory cost and discontinuity/high-frequency evidence.
access: Official NeurIPS 2021 OpenReview entry and open PDF verified. Optional failure-driven architecture.

title: Riesz Neural Operator for Solving Partial Differential Equations
authors: Shouyi Liu, Xiaokang Yang, Yuntian Chen
why: RNO mixes global spectra with local directional derivative information through the Riesz transform. That targets the local non-stationarity and sharp fronts that the current family-OOD test exposes.
extract: Extract spectral derivative block, directional features, local/global ablations, accepted benchmark scope and implementation cost for 3D data.
access: Official ICLR 2026 accepted OpenReview page and PDF verified. Advanced; do not present its PDE results as BOST evidence.

title: Neural Operator Learning for Ultrasound Tomography Inversion
authors: Haocheng Dai, Michael Penwarden, Robert M. Kirby, Sarang Joshi
venue: Medical Imaging with Deep Learning (MIDL), short paper
why: This is a close inverse-tomography analogue: a learned operator maps time-of-flight measurements directly to a heterogeneous sound-speed field generated by a full-wave solver.
extract: Extract observation/field interfaces, simulation-to-training pipeline, direct-inversion latency, split design and the limitations of a short proof-of-concept paper.
access: Official MIDL 2023 OpenReview paper and PDF verified.

title: Learned Primal-Dual Reconstruction
authors: Jonas Adler, Ozan Oktem
why: Learned primal-dual reconstruction embeds a possibly nonlinear forward operator into an unrolled reconstruction network. It is the canonical warning against treating tomography as generic voxel post-processing.
extract: Extract primal/dual state updates, forward/adjoint calls, raw-measurement interface, unroll depth, training cost and comparison with filtered backprojection.
access: PubMed record and DOI verified. Publisher/metadata link only in the public library.

title: Noise2Inverse: Self-Supervised Deep Convolutional Denoising for Tomography
authors: Allard A. Hendriksen, Daniel M. Pelt, K. Joost Batenburg
why: Noise2Inverse turns measurement partitions into statistically independent reconstructions and trains without clean targets. It is a foundational source for support/query view splitting.
extract: Extract independence and zero-mean assumptions, partition construction, bias argument, real experimental validation and failure cases when the noise model is wrong.
access: Official CWI record, DOI and open final-version PDF verified.

title: Equivariant Splitting: Self-supervised learning from incomplete data
authors: Victor Sechaud, Jeremy Scanvic, Quentin Barthelemy, Patrice Abry, Julian Tachella
why: This accepted self-supervised inverse-imaging paper combines splitting and equivariance for one incomplete observation model and includes sparse-view CT experiments.
extract: Extract support/query loss, equivariance assumptions, unbiased-estimator argument, rank-deficient forward models and what can or cannot transfer to BOST camera symmetries.
access: Official ICLR 2026 accepted OpenReview page and open PDF verified.

title: NAF: Neural Attenuation Fields for Sparse-View CBCT Reconstruction
authors: Ruyi Zha, Yanhao Zhang, Hongdong Li
venue: Medical Image Computing and Computer Assisted Intervention (MICCAI)
why: NAF is a self-supervised per-instance coordinate field with differentiable projections and hash encoding. It is a CT-side neural-field neighbour of NeRIF, not a neural operator.
extract: Extract ray integration, coordinate encoding, optimization time, sparse-view protocol, external-data requirement and comparison with voxel methods.
access: Official MICCAI paper page and open arXiv PDF verified.

title: SNAF: Sparse-view CBCT Reconstruction with Neural Attenuation Fields
authors: Yu Fang, Lanzhuju Mei, Changjian Li, Yuan Liu, Wenping Wang, Zhiming Cui, Dinggang Shen
why: SNAF explicitly uses view augmentation to improve sparse-view neural attenuation fields. It motivates training-time view dropout and curriculum for BOST.
extract: Extract augmentation construction, 20-view regime, fine-detail metrics, ablations and whether the method generalizes beyond subject-specific optimization.
access: Open arXiv preprint verified. Treat as a preprint, not an accepted core result.

title: Geometry-Aware Attenuation Learning for Sparse-View CBCT Reconstruction
authors: Zhentao Liu, Yu Fang, Changjian Li, Han Wu, Yuan Liu, Dinggang Shen, Zhiming Cui
why: The encoder-backprojection-decoder design is the strongest direct structural reference for converting multiple 2D views into a geometry-aware 3D feature volume before reconstruction.
extract: Extract 2D feature encoder, geometry backprojection, multi-view aggregation, 3D decoder, 5/10/20-view tests, real-data split and code requirements.
access: Official repository identifies the IEEE TMI paper; DOI and open accepted-manuscript PDF verified. The DOI is 2024 early access and the final volume is 2025.

title: C2RV: Cross-Regional and Cross-View Learning for Sparse-View CBCT Reconstruction
authors: Yiqun Lin, Jiewen Yang, Hualiang Wang, Xinpeng Ding, Wei Zhao, Xiaomeng Li
venue: IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
why: C2RV argues that sparse views should not be processed equally and introduces scale-view cross-attention. This directly challenges a uniform-average BOST view encoder.
extract: Extract multi-scale volumetric features, view weighting, cross-regional consistency, generalization protocol, parameter budget and ablation of equal view aggregation.
access: Official CVF CVPR 2024 page and open accepted paper verified.

title: Generalizable Structure-Aware INF: Biplanar-View CT Reconstruction via Disentangled Implicit Neural Field
authors: Bei Huang, Yuru Pei
why: GSA-INF combines a learned triplane volume prior with an implicit decoder for one- or two-view reconstruction. It is a useful population-prior plus per-instance-field design reference.
extract: Extract triplane latent prior, conditional inference, mask supervision, online adaptation, structure metrics and the medical-anatomy assumptions that do not transfer to flames.
access: Official ACCV/CVF page and open PDF verified.

title: Dynamic CT Reconstruction From Limited Views With Implicit Neural Representations and Parametric Motion Fields
authors: Albert W. Reed, Hyojin Kim, Rushil Anirudh, K. Aditya Mohan, Kyle Champley, Jingu Kang, Suren Jayasuriya
venue: IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV)
why: This 4D-CT work uses a template, parametric motion field and self-supervised sinogram consistency. It is a strong conceptual neighbour for a future dynamic BOST extension.
extract: Extract template/motion separation, analysis-by-synthesis loss, limited-view timing, continuous time queries and motion-model failure cases.
access: Official ICCV/CVF page, open paper, supplement and code links verified.

title: Fast and accurate sparse-view CBCT reconstruction using meta-learned neural attenuation field and hash-encoding regularization
authors: Heejun Shin, Taehee Kim, Jongho Lee, Se Young Chun, Seungryung Cho, Dongmyung Shin
why: FACT meta-trains a neural attenuation field to accelerate optimization on a new volume. It is a direct precedent for operator or population-prior warm-start of NeRIF.
extract: Extract meta-training set size, adaptation steps, hash regularization, cross-vendor/body evaluation and total wall-clock comparison.
access: Open arXiv preprint verified. Treat as design inspiration until publication status is separately confirmed.

title: Spatial Mixture-of-Experts
authors: Nikoli Dryden, Torsten Hoefler
venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
why: SMoE provides spatial routing, a self-supervised routing loss and expert-error damping. These are concrete tools for preventing a residual/absolute BOST router from collapsing.
extract: Extract routing granularity, load balance, self-supervised target, expert damping, collapse diagnostics and compute overhead.
access: Official NeurIPS 2022 proceedings page, paper and supplement verified.

title: Neural Experts: Mixture of Experts for Implicit Neural Representations
authors: Yizhak Ben-Shabat, Chamin Hewa Koneputugodage, Sameera Ramasinghe, Stephen Gould
venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
why: Neural Experts learns local piecewise-continuous implicit fields with a gate and includes conditioning/pretraining strategies. It may help a later NeRIF thin-front expert model.
extract: Extract gate pretraining, expert partitioning, convergence and continuity artifacts; separate local spatial experts from condition-wise residual/absolute experts.
access: Official NeurIPS 2024 OpenReview entry and PDF verified. Advanced INR neighbour.

title: Mixture-of-Experts Operator Transformer for Large-Scale PDE Pre-Training
authors: Hong Wang, Haiyang Xin, Jie Wang, Xuanze Yang, Fei Zha, Huanshuo Dong, Yan Jiang
venue: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems
why: MoE-POT shows a neural-operator router selecting equation- and dataset-specific experts. T16 only needs the routing logic, not its 30M-to-0.5B pretraining scale.
extract: Extract routed/shared experts, router interpretability, expert usage, activated parameters and the scale mismatch with an undergraduate BOST model.
access: Official NeurIPS 2025 accepted OpenReview page and PDF verified.

title: Probabilistic neural operators for functional uncertainty quantification
authors: Christopher Bulte, Philipp Scholl, Gitta Kutyniok
why: PNO learns distributions over output function spaces with proper scoring rules. It defines the ambitious endpoint for field-level uncertainty rather than a single voxel confidence map.
extract: Extract functional scoring rule, sampling cost, calibration evaluation, deterministic baselines and suitability for inverse reconstruction rather than forecasting.
access: Official accepted TMLR OpenReview page and open PDF verified.

title: Linearization Turns Neural Operators into Function-Valued Gaussian Processes
authors: Emilia Magnani, Marvin Pfortner, Tobias Weber, Philipp Hennig
why: LUNO adds structured predictive uncertainty to an already trained neural operator through linearization and a function-valued Gaussian process view.
extract: Extract linearization point, covariance approximation, memory/runtime, calibration metrics and whether uncertainty identifies out-of-distribution failures.
access: Official ICML 2025 spotlight OpenReview page and PDF verified.

title: Approximate Bayesian Neural Operators: Uncertainty Quantification for Parametric PDEs
authors: Emilia Magnani, Nicholas Kramer, Runa Eschenhagen, Lorenzo Rosasco, Philipp Hennig
why: This Bayesian treatment explicitly tests whether structured uncertainty identifies cases where a neural operator fails. That is the reliability claim T16 ultimately needs.
extract: Extract shallow GP formulation, approximate deep method, failure-detection plots, calibration and compute overhead relative to deep ensembles.
access: Official accepted TMLR OpenReview page and open PDF verified.

title: Geometric Operator Learning with Optimal Transport
authors: Xinyi Li, Zongyi Li, Nikola Kovachki, Anima Anandkumar
why: The method learns instance-dependent geometry embeddings through optimal transport. It is relevant only if BOST camera/ray geometries vary enough that a single latent grid is inadequate.
extract: Extract geometry density representation, instance-dependent deformation, 3D surface compression, memory cost and comparison with shared deformations.
access: Official JMLR 2025 page and open PDF verified.

title: Reconstruction of three-dimensional turbulent flows from sparse and noisy planar measurements: a weight-sharing neural network approach
authors: Yaxin Mo, Luca Magri
why: This open experimental-style flow reconstruction work trains without complete 3D ground truth, uses unseen sensor planes and physics loss, and directly addresses sparse/noisy three-dimensional flow recovery.
extract: Extract weight sharing, sensor layout, unseen-plane validation, physics/sensor model selection, five-run stability, energy spectrum and reported artifacts.
access: Official Cambridge open-access article and DOI verified. The HTML contains the full paper; public PDF URL is not hard-coded here.

title: The Differentiation of Pseudo-Inverses and Nonlinear Least Squares Problems Whose Variables Separate
authors: G. H. Golub, V. Pereyra
why: This classic variable-projection result formalizes eliminating linear least-squares coefficients before optimizing the remaining nonlinear variables. T16's one-dimensional support-fit weight is a much simpler special numerical step, not a novel neural contribution.
extract: Extract the separable-variable principle, pseudo-inverse assumptions, rank conditions and the distinction between a standard numerical elimination step and the surrounding BOST model contribution.
access: Official SIAM journal page, volume 10 issue 2, pages 413-432 and DOI verified. Publisher access only.

title: Deep Null Space Learning for Inverse Problems: Convergence Analysis and Rates
authors: Johannes Schwab, Stephan Antholzer, Markus Haltmeier
why: The paper constructs a neural correction projected into the forward-operator null space so that data consistency is preserved. It is the clearest mathematical precedent for SC-DBNO's proposed support-nullspace correction.
extract: Extract L = Id + (Id - A^+A)N, the data-consistency identity, regularization assumptions, approximate projector discussion and what breaks for nonlinear or changing BOST geometry.
access: Final Inverse Problems DOI and open arXiv manuscript verified; the open manuscript is used as the readable route.

title: Siamese Cooperative Learning for Unsupervised Image Reconstruction From Incomplete Measurements
authors: Yuhui Quan, Xinran Qin, Tongyao Pang, Hui Ji
venue: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence
why: Its twin networks cooperate across the measurement range and null space using only incomplete measurements. This is closer to the proposed support-fit plus query/nullspace decomposition than generic MoE routing papers.
extract: Extract range/null-space decomposition, three self-supervised losses, assumptions on the measurement matrix, identifiability, four imaging tasks and comparison with simpler splitting baselines.
access: IEEE TPAMI volume 46 issue 7 pages 4866-4879 and DOI verified via PubMed/DBLP; author-hosted readable manuscript verified.

title: MoDL: Model-Based Deep Learning Architecture for Inverse Problems
authors: Hemant K. Aggarwal, Merry P. Mani, Mathews Jacob
why: MoDL alternates a learned prior with explicit numerical data-consistency blocks. It is a strong architectural precedent for keeping the BOST forward model as an optimization layer rather than asking a free router to learn physics implicitly.
extract: Extract the alternating objective, conjugate-gradient data-consistency layer, weight sharing, arbitrary-forward-model claim, memory/runtime accounting and MRI-specific assumptions that do not transfer to BOST.
access: Final IEEE TMI metadata and DOI verified; NIH author manuscript, open arXiv PDF and official code repository are readable.

title: Variable-Input Deep Operator Networks
authors: Michael Prasthofer, Tim De Ryck, Siddhartha Mishra
why: VIDON permits a random number and location of sensors and is permutation invariant, making it a direct architecture reference when BOST camera counts or locations vary across samples.
extract: Extract the set encoder, sensor-location representation, universality assumptions, sampling distributions and fixed-sensor baselines; decide whether OERF geometry varies enough to justify it.
access: Open arXiv manuscript and metadata verified. It is a method component reference, not evidence on BOST rays.

title: GNOT: A General Neural Operator Transformer for Operator Learning
authors: Zhongkai Hao, Zhengyi Wang, Hang Su, Chengyang Ying, Yinpeng Dong, Songming Liu, Ze Cheng, Jian Song, Jun Zhu
why: GNOT handles multiple input functions, irregular meshes and multiscale domains through heterogeneous attention and geometric gating, matching the eventual displacement-plus-geometry input problem.
extract: Extract heterogeneous normalized attention, geometric gating, token scaling, compute cost and irregular-mesh ablations; do not adopt the full transformer before a simpler lift fails.
access: Official PMLR page and open conference PDF verified.

title: Operator Learning with Neural Fields: Tackling PDEs on General Geometries
authors: Louis Serrano, Lise Le Boudec, Armand Kassai Koupai, Thomas X. Wang, Yuan Yin, Jean-Noel Vittaut, Patrick Gallinari
why: CORAL uses coordinate neural fields inside operator learning and supports arbitrary sampling and geometry, providing a clean bridge between a population operator and a NeRIF-style coordinate decoder.
extract: Extract latent modulation, coordinate-field encoding/decoding, inverse-problem tests, resolution changes and training cost; map only the representation layer to BOST.
access: Official NeurIPS abstract page and open conference PDF verified.

title: Beyond Regular Grids: Fourier-Based Neural Operators on Arbitrary Domains
authors: Levi E. Lingsch, Mike Yan Michelis, Emmanuel De Bezenac, Sirani M. Perera, Robert K. Katzschmann, Siddhartha Mishra
why: The method directly evaluates spectral transforms on non-equispaced point sets, offering an alternative to interpolation or graph-to-grid lifting for irregular BOST geometries.
extract: Extract transform complexity, quadrature/sampling assumptions, arbitrary-domain benchmarks and comparison with graph or interpolation baselines.
access: Official PMLR page and open ICML PDF verified.

title: Latent Neural Operator for Solving Forward and Inverse PDE Problems
authors: Tian Wang, Chuang Wang
why: Physics-Cross-Attention maps irregular geometric observations into a compact latent space and decodes arbitrary query positions, which is relevant if BOST ray and voxel tokens become too large.
extract: Extract PhCA, latent token count, inverse benchmark, interpolation/extrapolation protocol, memory accounting and arbitrary-query decoder.
access: Official NeurIPS page, PDF and DOI verified.

title: Implicit Representations via Operator Learning
authors: Sourav Pal, Harshavardhan Adepu, Clinton Wang, Polina Golland, Vikas Singh
why: O-INR gives an operator-theoretic formulation of implicit representations, helping separate a cross-sample function-space prior from per-instance coordinate optimization.
extract: Extract the integral-transform formulation, convolutional implementation, downstream INR tasks and limits of the neighborhood interpretation.
access: Official PMLR page, open ICML PDF and public code link verified.

title: Neural Operators with Localized Integral and Differential Kernels
authors: Miguel Liu-Schiaffini, Julius Berner, Boris Bonev, Thorsten Kurth, Kamyar Azizzadenesheli, Anima Anandkumar
why: The work targets FNO over-smoothing by combining global spectral layers with localized integral and differential kernels, directly addressing T16's thin-front failure signature.
extract: Extract resolution-convergent local kernels, matched FNO ablations, local/global fusion and irregular-grid assumptions. Re-test every gain on independently generated BOST fields.
access: ICML publication identity, official NVIDIA page and open arXiv manuscript verified.

title: DuFal: Dual-Frequency-Aware Learning for High-Fidelity Extremely Sparse-view CBCT Reconstruction
authors: Cuong Tran Van, Trong-Thang Pham, Ngoc-Son Nguyen, Duy Minh Ho Nguyen, Ngan Le
why: DuFal combines global and local high-frequency FNO branches for extremely sparse-view CBCT, offering a concrete high-frequency architecture neighbor for thin fronts.
extract: Extract local patching, spectral-channel factorization, frequency fusion, sparse-view protocol and compute cost; keep CT attenuation conclusions separate from BOS deflection.
access: Official OpenReview page identifies the work as accepted by TMLR and provides an open PDF and code link.

title: NPN: Non-Linear Projections of the Null-Space for Imaging Inverse Problems
authors: Roman Jacome, Romario Gualdron-Hurtado, Leon Suarez, Henry Arguello
why: NPN is the closest novelty overlap: it explicitly learns low-dimensional projections and neural coefficients tied to the sensing matrix null space across several imaging inverse problems.
extract: Extract the projection construction, orthogonality constraints, coefficient network, convergence assumptions and CT/MRI protocols. Use it as a mandatory low-rank null-space baseline, not as evidence that LGWO has learned a BOST null space.
access: NeurIPS 2025 status, current arXiv v2 abstract, open manuscript and author-linked code were verified from the primary arXiv record.

title: Neural Operators Meet Conjugate Gradients: The FCG-NO Method for Efficient PDE Solving
authors: Alexander Rudikov, Vladimir Fanaskov, Ekaterina Muravleva, Yuri M. Laevsky, Ivan Oseledets
why: FCG-NO already combines a discretization-invariant neural operator with flexible conjugate gradients and nonlinear preconditioning, so LGWO cannot claim the generic neural-operator-plus-Krylov combination as new.
extract: Extract why nonlinear learned preconditioners require FCG, the training loss, resolution-transfer tests, classical preconditioner controls and full iteration/runtime accounting. Build an FCG-NO-style proxy only under matched operator-call budgets.
access: Official ICML 2024 PMLR record and open PMLR PDF verified.

title: Invertible Fourier Neural Operators for Tackling Both Forward and Inverse Problems
authors: Da Long, Zhitong Xu, Qiwei Yuan, Yin Yang, Shandian Zhe
why: iFNO is a direct inverse-operator competitor that shares invertible latent blocks between forward and inverse maps and adds a VAE for ill-posedness, data scarcity and noise.
extract: Extract the invertible blocks, VAE posterior, three-stage training, seven benchmark splits and code assumptions. Compare LGWO only on matched data, field/reprojection/tail metrics and end-to-end cost.
access: Official AISTATS 2025 PMLR record, open PMLR PDF and official code link verified.

title: A Neural-Preconditioned Poisson Solver for Mixed Dirichlet and Neumann Boundary Conditions
authors: Kai Weixian Lan, Elias Gueidon, Ayano Kaneda, Julian Panetta, Joseph Teran
why: Lan et al. provide a strong precedent for a lightweight learned approximate inverse used inside an iterative solver under changing geometry, boundary conditions and grid size.
extract: Extract the spatially varying kernels, setup-versus-per-solve cost, unseen geometry/grid tests and algebraic multigrid comparison. Keep Poisson preconditioning distinct from a single BOST first-direction proposal.
access: Official ICML 2024 PMLR record and open PMLR PDF verified.

title: Neural Correction Operator: A Reliable and Fast Approach for Electrical Impedance Tomography
authors: Amit Bhat, Ke Chen, Chunmei Wang
why: A limited-iteration physics reconstruction followed by a learned correction is the closest component-level precedent for T16's lift-plus-correction strategy in a severely ill-posed inverse problem.
extract: Extract the reconstruction/correction composition, direct-operator controls, equal-architecture comparisons, noise tests and L-BFGS iteration/runtime budget.
access: Final JCP DOI and publisher metadata plus open arXiv manuscript and public code link verified.

title: Calibrated Uncertainty Quantification for Operator Learning via Conformal Prediction
authors: Ziqi Ma, David Pitt, Kamyar Azizzadenesheli, Anima Anandkumar
why: This work provides finite-sample functional calibration for operator outputs, a principled upgrade from uncalibrated ensemble variance when T16 reaches an independent calibration set.
extract: Extract the risk-controlling quantile operator, functional coverage definition, calibration split, 3D test and finite-sample assumptions.
access: Official TMLR OpenReview page and open PDF verified; arXiv 2402.01960 is the readable preprint route.

title: Conformalized-DeepONet: A Distribution-Free Framework for Uncertainty Quantification in Deep Operator Networks
authors: Christian Moya, Amirhossein Mollaali, Zecheng Zhang, Lu Lu, Guang Lin
why: Split conformal prediction supplies distribution-free prediction intervals for DeepONet regression and is a practical UQ baseline for coordinate-wise or functional bands.
extract: Extract split calibration, score definitions, interval construction, coverage claims and differences between pointwise and simultaneous field guarantees.
access: Final Physica D DOI, publisher metadata and open arXiv manuscript verified.

title: SelectiveNet: A Deep Neural Network with an Integrated Reject Option
authors: Yonatan Geifman, Ran El-Yaniv
why: SelectiveNet formalizes reject-option learning and risk-coverage trade-offs, which T16 and M3B need when abstention or NeRIF fallback is treated as a system decision.
extract: Extract coverage constraints, selective risk, auxiliary loss, calibration procedure and why fallback performance must be audited separately.
access: Official PMLR page and 9-page open ICML PDF verified and cached from the proceedings site on 2026-07-16.

title: Harnessing the Power of Neural Operators with Automatically Encoded Conservation Laws
authors: Ning Liu, Yiming Fan, Xianyi Zeng, Milan Kloewer, Lu Zhang, Yue Yu
why: ClawNO hard-encodes conservation laws and is relevant only if the project later predicts temporal flow variables with a valid continuity law, not merely a refractive-index volume.
extract: Extract divergence-free construction, compatible variables, small-data results and assumptions. Explicitly test whether the target BOST-derived quantity obeys the required law.
access: Official PMLR page and open ICML PDF verified.

title: Learned Initializations for Optimizing Coordinate-Based Neural Representations
authors: Matthew Tancik, Ben Mildenhall, Terrance Wang, Divi Schmidt, Pratul P. Srinivasan, Jonathan T. Barron, Ren Ng
why: Direct precedent for learning initial weights of coordinate-based neural representations to accelerate per-instance fitting and improve partial-observation generalization; it defines the strongest prior-art boundary for a NeRIF warm-start claim.
extract: Extract the meta-learning objective, MAML/Reptile protocol, time-to-quality curves, CT/3D inverse experiments, partial-observation tests and which weights are shared versus adapted.
access: Official CVF proceedings page and 10-page open accepted-version PDF verified. The paper is not BOST and its learned initialization is not measurement-conditioned.

title: Learning Transferable Features for Implicit Neural Representations
authors: Kushal Vyas, Ahmed Imtiaz Humayun, Aniket Dashpute, Richard G. Baraniuk, Ashok Veeraraghavan, Guha Balakrishnan
why: STRAINER transfers shared encoder features as initialization for fitting new INRs, supplying a modern comparison for shared-prior or learned-initialization NeRIF experiments.
extract: Extract the shared-encoder/independent-decoder split, initialization protocol, in-domain and OOD fitting, inverse-problem tests, early time-to-quality gain and transfer failure boundaries.
access: Official NeurIPS 2024 proceedings page, DOI and Google Research metadata verified. Its image-domain early gain must not be extrapolated to volumetric NeRIF.

title: An Iteration Formula for Fredholm Integral Equations of the First Kind
authors: Louis Landweber
why: This is the classical origin of the adjoint-gradient iteration that v3k-C now treats as a required numerical baseline. Reading it prevents a standard solver from being mislabeled as a neural contribution.
extract: Extract the iteration, operator assumptions and convergence role. Re-derive the finite-dimensional step-size condition and distinguish convergence of the data objective from recovery of the true field.
access: DOI and JSTOR issue record verified on 2026-07-15. Publisher/metadata link only; no subscription PDF is cached.

title: Solving Ill-Posed Inverse Problems Using Iterative Deep Neural Networks
authors: Jonas Adler, Ozan Oktem
why: The paper is a direct prior-art boundary for learned adjoint-residual updates: it feeds data-discrepancy and regularizer gradients into a partially learned iterative scheme for nonlinear inverse problems.
extract: Track the forward operator, data gradient, regularizer gradient, learned update, iteration state and training target. List which CT-specific claims do not transfer to BOST.
